title: brachyscome tetrapterocarpa
intro: Brachyscome tetrapterocarpa, also known as the small white daisy, is a species of flowering plant in the daisy family native to Australia. It has been used traditionally for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help to soothe skin irritations and wounds. The plant has been found to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing, as well as exhibiting antioxidant and antiviral activities. Brachyscome tetrapterocarpa can be prepared as a herbal infusion, decoction, or tincture, and is often used in traditional medicine to treat various conditions, including skin infections and respiratory issues.
